item.title: The pop factory
item.description: Theodor Adorno argued in On Popular Music that the culture industry standardizes its products: every hit follows a pre-given pattern, and the variations between individual songs are surface differences designed to mask that uniformity.

item.title: Modal interchange
item.description: Also known as: modal mixture, borrowed chords First heard in: Western classical harmony; became a defining harmonic texture in 1960s pop and rock through the Beatles, Brian Wilson, and Burt Bacharach Related concepts: I–vi–IV–V progression, Pentatonic and blues scales The coda of “Hey Jude” (1968) r...

item.title: LIBRARY
item.description: The scholarly backbone. Condensed arguments of the books and essays the MAP thinks with.
